name: Azure Updates MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server monitoring the Azure Updates RSS feed to provide AI agents with the latest Microsoft Azure service announcements, preview releases, general availability milestones, and deprecation notices. Enables agents to stay current on Azure platform changes without manual monitoring.
use_cases: Monitoring Azure service announcements for cloud architecture agents
Tracking Azure GA releases and deprecations for DevOps automation agents
Alerting teams to relevant Azure updates from Azure monitoring agents
Building Azure change management workflows informed by update feeds
not_for: AWS or GCP update monitoring (Azure-specific RSS feed)
Real-time Azure service health (use Azure Service Health API instead)
Detailed technical documentation (update feed provides summaries)
best_when: A cloud architect or DevOps team using Azure wants AI agents to monitor Azure update announcements — surfacing relevant platform changes for architecture planning and upgrade decisions.
avoid_when: You use AWS or GCP. This is Azure-specific. For service health incidents, use Azure Service Health API directly.

auth.notes: No auth required. Reads publicly available Azure Updates RSS feed.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — reads public Azure RSS feed
pricing.notes: Free open source. Azure Updates RSS feed is publicly available.

performance.rate_limits: RSS feed polling — be respectful with polling frequency

known_agent_gotchas: RSS feed content is summary-level — not a substitute for full Azure documentation
Update relevance filtering is important — Azure updates many services simultaneously
Poll frequency should be reasonable — hourly is fine, per-minute polling could be flagged
Community implementation — validate against direct Azure Updates page for critical info
agent_readiness.security_notes: Read-only public RSS feed. No auth required. No sensitive data involved. Low security risk.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public RSS feed access. No auth. No sensitive data. Very low security risk.
